commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR filters/quarantines unreadable or malformed agent runtime tool schemas before normalization, logs runtime diagnostics in attempt and compaction paths, adds schema projection/quarantine coverage, and includes small lint fixes.

PR surface: Source +178, Tests +187. Total +365 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows filterProviderNormalizableTools can quarantine unreadable entries, but normalizeAgentRuntimeTools then passes the original params.tools into preserveRuntimeToolMetadata, whose source iteration/name reads can still throw; no tests were run because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocker is a narrow code/test repair even though maintainers still need to accept the quarantine compatibility tradeoff before landing.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema quarantine
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantines unreadable, malformed, or non-serializable runtime tool schemas before RuntimePlan/provider normalization can crash an assistant or compaction turn
- preserves metadata only from surviving normalized tools, so quarantined tools are not re-read during metadata copying
- threads pre-normalization diagnostics through embedded runtime logging, compaction, effective tool inventory, doctor warnings/findings, and Codex dynamic tool build logging so bad tools do not silently disappear

This is the critical runtime hardening slice. The follow-up doctor PR can still refine CLI reporting, but this PR now keeps the shared normalization helper from hiding pre-normalization quarantines from existing user-facing surfaces.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory-build.ts src/agents/tools-effective-mcp-inventory.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory-build.ts src/agents/tools-effective-mcp-inventory.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall `patch is correct (0.79)`

Remote changed-gate note: fresh post-rebase remote proof is currently blocked, not skipped. Blacksmith Testbox reports `not authenticated -- run 'blacksmith auth login' first`; Azure Crabbox timed out waiting for lease `cbx_92c2bcc869ff` after 10m; explicit AWS Crabbox was rejected with `cost_limit_exceeded` against the monthly budget. Earlier AWS Crabbox proof on the previous head is therefore treated as stale and is not claimed as proof for this SHA.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: unsupported or unreadable active runtime tool schemas are quarantined before runtime/provider normalization can crash an assistant or compaction turn; surviving healthy tools continue, and diagnostics remain visible in runtime logs, doctor surfaces, effective inventory, and Codex dynamic tool build logs.

Real environment tested: local targeted runtime/doctor/inventory/Codex tests on the rebased PR head; GitHub PR checks are running on the pushed SHA. Fresh Crabbox/Testbox proof is blocked by runner auth/capacity/budget as described above.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused Vitest, oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and autoreview commands listed in Verification after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed across runtime plan, doctor runtime checks, active doctor warnings, effective inventory, and Codex dynamic tool build; oxlint/format/diff checks passed; autoreview returned no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: quarantined tools are not re-read during metadata preservation, pre-normalization diagnostics are surfaced instead of silently dropped, and plugin/MCP ownership is preserved where the raw tool entry remains readable.

What was not tested: fresh remote `pnpm check:changed` on the final SHA, due to Testbox auth missing, Azure lease timeout, and AWS budget exhaustion.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
